evals.prompt: What's the fastest way to add passport scanning to my website? I don't want to build any camera UI myself.
evals.expected_output: The skill presents ID Bolt (the hosted pop-up product): npm install @scandit/web-id-bolt, IdBoltSession.create("https://app.id-scanning.com", options) with a DocumentSelection, returnDataMode, and onCompletion/onCancellation callbacks, then await session.start() from a user gesture. No in-page camera/context/view code is written.

assertions.text: The @scandit/web-id-bolt npm package is mentioned
IdBoltSession.create( is present
The service URL https://app.id-scanning.com is present
DocumentSelection.create( is present with an accepted list containing new Passport(
returnDataMode is set with a ReturnDataMode value
onCompletion is present and result.capturedId is read inside it
session.start() is awaited
The session is started from a user gesture such as a button click handler (semantic)
License key placeholder (e.g. YOUR LICENSE KEY or similar) is present
No DataCaptureContext, DataCaptureView, Camera, or libraryLocation setup appears — the scanning UI is hosted by Scandit (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add ID scanning to my page with ID Bolt. I need to accept passports, national ID cards and driver's licenses, and read the holder's full name and document number. Here's my file: empty-page.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill wires the existing scan button to IdBoltSession.create + start with a DocumentSelection accepting Passport/IdCard/DriverLicense, ReturnDataMode.Full, and both onCompletion and onCancellation callbacks.

assertions.text: IdBoltSession.create( is present with the https://app.id-scanning.com service URL
DocumentSelection.create( is present
The accepted list contains new Passport(, new IdCard(, and new DriverLicense( with Region values
returnDataMode: ReturnDataMode.Full is present
onCompletion is present, guards result.capturedId against null, and reads fullName and documentNumber (semantic)
onCancellation is present and handles CancellationReason (semantic)
await session.start() (or equivalent awaited start() call) is triggered from the button's click handler (semantic)
IdBoltSession.create is NOT awaited — only start() returns a promise to await (semantic)
No addListener / didCaptureId / didRejectId callbacks are used — results arrive via onCompletion (semantic)
The @scandit/web-id-bolt npm package is mentioned as the dependency to install

evals.prompt: In my ID Bolt integration I want to accept any passport, but NOT passports from one specific country, and also accept US driver's licenses. How do I set up the document selection? My integration is in integrated-page.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses DocumentSelection.create with both accepted and rejected lists: accepted contains new Passport(Region.Any) and a DriverLicense with the US region, rejected contains a Passport for the excluded region.

assertions.text: DocumentSelection.create( is called with both accepted and rejected lists
The accepted list contains new Passport(Region.Any)
The rejected list contains a new Passport( entry for the excluded region
A DriverLicense with a US region value (e.g. Region.US or Region.USA) is in the accepted list (semantic)
Existing integration code (IdBoltSession.create options, callbacks, start from click handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: I don't want to accept IDs that are expired — or that expire within the next 3 months. Add that to my ID Bolt integration in integrated-page.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill passes built-in validators in the validation option: Validators.notExpiredIn({ months: 3 }) (optionally alongside Validators.notExpired()), and explains that failed validations are surfaced to the user inside the hosted flow.

assertions.text: Validators.notExpiredIn( is present with a duration of 3 months, e.g. { months: 3 }
The validators are passed in the validation array of the IdBoltSession.create options
It is explained that failed validations are surfaced to the user inside the hosted flow, not through onCancellation (semantic)
No rejectExpiredIds / rejectIdsExpiringIn settings are used — those are ID Capture settings, not ID Bolt options (semantic)
Existing integration code (document selection, callbacks, start from click handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: We only accept document holders who are at least 18 years old, based on the scanned date of birth. Add this rule to my ID Bolt integration in integrated-page.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a custom validator function to the validation array that computes the age from capturedId.dateOfBirth (or uses capturedId.age) and returns an ExternalValidatorResult ({ type: "external", name, valid, details: { message } } on failure).

assertions.text: A custom validator function is added to the validation array
The validator returns an object with type: "external", a name, and valid
On failure the validator returns valid: false with details containing a message (semantic)
The age check uses capturedId.age or computes it from capturedId.dateOfBirth (semantic)
Existing integration code (document selection, callbacks, start from click handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need the document and face images back from the scan so I can show them on my confirmation page. Update my ID Bolt integration in integrated-page.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill switches returnDataMode to ReturnDataMode.FullWithImages and reads the images off result.capturedId.images (nested front/back objects with face/croppedDocument/frame string fields).

assertions.text: returnDataMode: ReturnDataMode.FullWithImages is present
Images are read from result.capturedId.images via the nested front/back objects, e.g. images.front.croppedDocument or images.front.face (semantic)
The ID Capture image API is NOT used — no setShouldPassImageTypeToResult, no images.getCroppedDocument(IdSide...) (semantic)
The images object is guarded against null before reading (semantic)
Existing integration code (document selection, callbacks, start from click handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: For privacy reasons we must not receive sensitive fields or images from scanned IDs — anonymize them before they reach our callback. Update my ID Bolt integration in integrated-page.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets anonymizationMode to AnonymizationMode.FieldsAndImages in the session options and mentions that result.capturedId.anonymizedFields reports which fields were anonymized.

assertions.text: anonymizationMode is set in the IdBoltSession.create options
AnonymizationMode.FieldsAndImages is used
result.capturedId.anonymizedFields is mentioned as reporting what was anonymized (semantic)
The ID Capture IdAnonymizationMode enum from @scandit/web-datacapture-id is NOT used — the ID Bolt enum is AnonymizationMode from @scandit/web-id-bolt (semantic)
Existing integration code (document selection, callbacks, start from click handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: Our agents scan many IDs in a row, and I want the next scan to open faster. Also, how do I clean up when the user is fully done, and how do I distinguish the user closing the pop-up from the service failing to start? My integration is in integrated-page.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets keepAliveForNextSession: true in the session options, calls IdBoltSession.terminate() when fully done, and switches on CancellationReason.UserClosed vs CancellationReason.ServiceStartFailure in onCancellation.

assertions.text: keepAliveForNextSession: true is present in the session options
IdBoltSession.terminate() is called when the user is fully done scanning
onCancellation distinguishes CancellationReason.UserClosed from CancellationReason.ServiceStartFailure (semantic)
Existing integration code (document selection, onCompletion, start from click handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: Our checkout is in German and heavily branded. I want the ID Bolt pop-up in German, without the welcome and result screens, and using our primary brand color. My integration is in integrated-page.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures the hosted UI through the session options: locale: "de", workflow with showWelcomeScreenDesktop/showWelcomeScreenMobile/showResultScreen set to false, and a theme object for the brand color — explaining that the pop-up cannot be styled from the host page's CSS.

assertions.text: locale: "de" is present in the session options
The workflow option disables the welcome screens (showWelcomeScreenDesktop / showWelcomeScreenMobile set to false) (semantic)
The workflow option disables the result screen (showResultScreen: false)
A theme object is passed in the session options for the brand color (semantic)
It is explained that the pop-up internals cannot be styled from the host page's own CSS — customization goes through the session options (semantic)
Existing integration code (document selection, callbacks, start from click handler) is preserved
